steps.title: Spot the key difference in the evidence
steps.content: The passage notes that captive primate studies show significantly more right-handedness than wild primate studies, and this difference “raises the possibility that” something explains it.

steps.title: Match the explanation to the difference
steps.content: C provides the causal mechanism: exposure to humans in captivity would lead primates to adopt more right-handed behaviors, directly explaining why captive groups show higher right-handedness than wild groups. The other choices address sample size or methodology but don't explain the captive-versus-wild pattern. That matches choice C — C is correct.
